payload.claim_text: By the end of 2017, NYC DOT is ready to implement TSP along the M60 SBS, Q44 SBS, B46 SBS (extension), S62/S92, Q25 and Q43 upon the MTA's procurement of new bus technology.
By the end of 2017, NYC DOT will be ready to implement 229 additional intersections on 5 additional TSP routes, contingent on MTA's planned procurement of additional bus technology.

As the MTA will soon have the TSP technology available for all buses, NYC DOT will accelerate its implementation of TSP, expanding the network after 2017 to an additional 550 intersections (about 10 routes) by end of 2020.
NYC DOT will accelerate its implementation of TSP, expanding the network by an additional 550 intersections (about 10 routes) by the end of 2020, in concert with MTA's new bus technology.

As of July 2017, TSP is being planned on the following corridors: Main Street - Q44 SBS, LaGuardia/125th Street - M60 SBS, Utica Avenue extension - B46 SBS, Victory Boulevard extension - S62/S92, Kissena Boulevard - Q25, Hillside Avenue - Q43, Merrick Boulevard - Q5, South Bronx - Bx6 SBS, Southern Brooklyn - B82, Woodhaven Boulevard - Q52/Q53 SBS, Fordham Road - Bx12 SBS
On average, TSP has reduced bus travel times about 14 percent during weekday peak morning and evening commuting periods.

This new generation of TSP has been implemented on 5 corridors (about 260 intersections) since 2012: M15 SBS/Lower Manhattan (2014), B44 SBS/Nostrand Avenue (2015), S79 SBS/Hylan Boulevard (2015), Bx41 SBS/Webster Avenue (2015), and most recently B46 SBS/Utica Avenue (2016).
Results vary by corridor, direction and time of day with travel time savings ranging from less than 1 percent to up to 25 percent.
As of June 2017, TSP is currently provided at 260 intersections on 5 bus routes.

description: SBS on Utica Avenue on the B46 bus route in Brooklyn
Select Bus Service on Utica Avenue. Proposed weekday frequency: 3 min AM Peak, 7 min Midday, 5 min PM Peak, 5 min Early Evening, 8 min Late Evening.

B46 Limited-Stop bus route in Brooklyn, operates 7 days a week. Limited stops between DeKalb Av/Malcolm X Blvd and Avenue H/Utica Av, all stops along rest of route.

B46 Select Bus Service, Brooklyn's busiest bus route with 38,000 daily riders. Launched July 2016. Connects Bed-Stuy to subway lines and 30 bus routes.
Existing B46 Limited-stop service running along Utica Avenue and Malcolm X Boulevard. Limited stops will either be converted to SBS stops or local stops.
Proposed B46 Select Bus Service on Utica Avenue. Northern terminal at DeKalb Av, making SBS stops from DeKalb Av to Avenue U (Kings Plaza). Will not make local stops south of Avenue H.

payload.gap_text: Correction: After publishing the TSP report in July 2017, we have since found it contains some data that is no longer accurate. The report has been updated.
payload.missing_information: The specific data that was inaccurate and the correction details are not stated.
